customManagers.description: Update Makefile tool versions via inline renovate comments
Track the kindest/node image (tag + digest) pinned in the Makefile
Track the mise version pinned in jdx/mise-action steps
Track the C4-PlantUML stdlib version pinned in diagram !include URLs

packageRules.description: Wait 3 days before automerging major updates
Group GitHub Actions into one PR
Bump Go version in go.mod to latest patch
Group the Go toolchain across go.mod + onboarding/Dockerfile + .mise.toml so a patch bump lands in one PR and check-go-alignment never deadlocks
Group Makefile-pinned tool versions into one PR

Disable digest pinning for the Makefile's docker-datasource pins (PLANTUML_VERSION, KIND_NODE_IMAGE). config:best-practices' docker:pinDigests otherwise generates a pinDigest update that collides with the version bump in renovate/makefile-tools and SILENTLY DROPS it (verified: plantuml 1.2026.2->1.2026.6 and kindest/node v1.35.0->v1.36.1 were both being dropped). Scoped by file+datasource (NOT matchManagers — the AND can void on the regex-vs-custom.regex name). KIND_NODE_IMAGE keeps its existing-digest refresh; this only suppresses pin-where-absent.

Hold mise aqua/github-tags tool bumps for 3 days so the upstream GitHub Release artifacts that mise's aqua: backend installs are published before the PR opens — prevents the tag-before-publish 404 across ALL aqua tools (act, dapr, hadolint, golangci-lint, trivy, gitleaks, actionlint, shellcheck, kind). Scoped to github-tags so go/node version pins are unaffected.

Pair the kind binary (.mise.toml, github-tags) with the kindest/node image (Makefile, docker) so a KinD bump and its matching node image land together — each KinD release publishes a matching node image tag.

kindest/node: take version bumps (each brings its own digest) but suppress standalone digest-refresh updates — a digest refresh of the current tag otherwise collides with and silently drops the version bump in the grouped branch (verified: v1.35.0->v1.36.1 was being dropped).
Group .NET Docker images, disable major updates
